title: Tangy Fluffy Venezuelan Fines-Herbes Empanadas
description: Flaky pastry pockets filled with a tangy and fluffy fines-herbes filling, perfect for a festive Venezuelan dinner. This recipe is low-fat friendly and easy to make.
instructions: Combine flour and salt in a large bowl.
Add cold butter and use a pastry blender to work it into the flour until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Gradually add ice water, stirring with a fork until the dough comes together in a ball.
Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead a few times until it becomes smooth and pliable.
Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
Roll out the dough on a lightly floured surface to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
Place a spoonful of the fines-herbes filling in the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough in half over the filling and press the edges together to seal the empanada.
Use a fork to crimp the edges and create a decorative border.
Place the empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Brush the tops with egg yolks and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
Repeat with the remaining dough and filling.
Serve hot with your favorite dipping sauce.
